title: CRISPR-Cas12a Biosensor Array for Ultrasensitive Detection of Unamplified DNA with Single-Nucleotide Polymorphic Discrimination
research_objectives: Develop amplification-free DNA detection platform using CRISPR-Cas12a
Achieve attomolar sensitivity for both ssDNA and dsDNA targets
Implement multiplexed 48-sensor array for reliable detection

research_scope.focus_area: CRISPR-based biosensing and nucleic acid detection
research_scope.methodology: Integration of CRISPR-Cas12a with graphene field-effect transistors

conventional_challenges: Nucleic acid amplification (NAA) requirement adds complexity
Long turnaround times from sample to result
problem_addressed.amplification_free_advantage: Circumvents NAA issues with direct detection

technical_approach.mechanism: Multiturn trans-cleavage activity for continuous signal generation
technical_approach.transduction: Graphene field-effect transistor converts enzymatic activity to electrical signal

specificity_capabilities.sequence_selectivity: Base pair complementarity for specific recognition
